response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and open-source development experience, particularly in geospatial data (GISCollective) and performance-critical systems (Sociomantic). However, his recent work leans heavily toward TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling, with limited evidence of modern C++ or Rust experience in production routing/tiling systems. His geospatial background is a strong fit, but the role's core requirements (C++/Rust, distributed systems) are less prominently featured in his recent work.
strengths: Extensive geospatial data experience (GISCollective, Green Map), directly relevant to routing pipelines.
Proven track record in performance-critical systems (e.g., low-latency apps at Sociomantic using DLang).
Strong open-source contributions and full-stack versatility, indicating adaptability to new domains.
concerns: Primary expertise in TypeScript/Node.js, with no clear evidence of recent C++ or Rust production work.
Limited explicit experience with distributed systems concepts (fault tolerance, consistency) in routing/tiling contexts.
Recent roles (re:cinq, Optilyz) focus on AI tooling and pricing automation, diverging from geospatial data pipelines.
